author: Andocides
criterion_label: Civic rights granted to those who serve the state
in_group: Those who have served the state
out_group: Those who have not served the state
resource: Civic rights and substantial grants of money
speaker: Andocides
verbatim: I often see you bestowing civic rights and substantial grants of money upon both slaves and foreigners from every part of the world, if they prove to have done you some service. And you are acting wisely in making such gifts; they engender the greatest possible willingness to serve you.
comment: Resource Public honors is not direct enoguht as a ressource

author: Andocides
criterion_label: Loss of civic rights due to past misconduct
speaker: Andocides
verbatim: You decreed on the motion of Menippus that I should be granted immunity; restore me my rights under that decree. The herald shall read it to you, as it is lying even now among the records in the Council-chamber. This decree to which you have been listening, gentlemen, was passed by you in my favour, but afterwards revoked to oblige another.
comment:
this is not cncrete enought; Resource
Lifelong honor and calm

author: Antiphon
criterion_label: Public duties imposed on wealthier citizens
speaker: Author
verbatim: When I was appointed Choregus for the Thargelia, the χορηγία was one of the λητουργίαι, or public duties, imposed upon the richer citizens by the state. A Choregus had to equip and train a chorus for one of the annual festivals, in this case the Thargelia, held in honor of Apollo and celebrated on the 7th of Thargelion (May 1st) by a competition between choirs of boys selected from the ten tribes, which were grouped in pairs for the purpose.
extraction_method: HYBRID_EXTRACTED (11 passages, 8 pages from original 14 pages)
comment:
not good enought: Resource
Social honor and recognition

author: Antiphon
speaker: Author
verbatim: As to the slaves, if he considered that they were answering his questions truthfully, well and good; if he did not, I was ready to place all my own at his disposal for examination under torture, and should he demand any that did not belong to me, I agreed to obtain the consent of their owner and hand them over to him to examine as he liked.
extraction_method: HYBRID_EXTRACTED (11 passages, 8 pages from original 14 pages)

author: Aeschines
speaker: Aeschines
verbatim: Aeschines insinuates that only by some extraordinary favoritism could Demosthenes, with his strain of Scythian blood, ever have been recognized as an Athenian of pure blood, and so enrolled in the citizen-list when he came to manhood.

author: Aeschylus
criterion_label: Inheritance rights based on legitimate lineage
resource: Inheritance and control over family estate
speaker: Orestes
verbatim: O father, who perished by a death unbefitting a king, grant in answer to my prayer the lordship over your halls! And I too, father, have a like request of you: to escape when I have wrought great destruction on Aegisthus. Yes, for then the customary funeral feasts o... As for the rest, since your heart is rightly set on action, put your fortune to the test and get to your work at once.
